name: Garlic-derived metabolite activates LKB1, promotes adipose eNAMPT secretion, and improves age-related muscle function via hypothalamic signaling
summary: A Cell Metabolism study reporting that S-1-propenyl-L-cysteine activates LKB1/SIRT1 signaling in white adipose tissue, promotes extracellular NAMPT secretion, improves skeletal-muscle force and frailty indices in aged mice through hypothalamic signaling, and increases circulating eNAMPT in human individuals with healthy adipose mass.
